items.title: Agent Harnesses: The New Control Plane
items.summary: Gartner predicts 20 AI agents per enterprise by 2027 — but 73% are ungoverned shadow agents. The engineering challenge of 2026 is controlling agents, not building them.
items.why: Agent sprawl is the new technical debt. Without harnesses, agents make autonomous decisions without oversight.

items.title: Perplexity's Model Council
items.summary: Multi-model orchestration solving the problem that no single model is best at everything.
items.why: Different tasks (coding, research, creative) have different best models.
items.takeaway: Build routing logic that picks the right model for each task.

items.title: Agent Skills Security Risks
items.summary: 341 malicious skills on ClawHub distributing macOS malware; 7.1% of skills leak hardcoded API keys.
items.why: Skills are powerful but dangerous when unvetted.
items.takeaway: Audit every skill before deployment. Never copy code with embedded secrets.

items.title: Agentic AI Reshaping Engineering Workflows
items.summary: AI moving from coding assistant to autonomous team member across the entire SDLC.
items.why: Weeks of coordination compressing into continuous workflows.
items.takeaway: Design agents as team members, not tools.

items.title: Small-Scale AI for Developing World
items.summary: World Bank says the real AI revolution is small, low-cost tools solving local problems.
items.takeaway: Sometimes the best AI solution is the simplest one.

experiment: Build a simple agent harness that enforces cost budget and tool allowlist

gaps: No automated security scanning for skills
Missing explicit cost tracking for agent runs
